title: fully hosted SaaS control plane, authentication, teams, collaboration, and persistence
trigger: the generic local and CI runner contract is stable and the redacted hosted-envelope gate passes

title: Git, GitHub, or Graphite transport and review replacement
trigger: the submitted semantic layer proves a transport limitation that cannot be handled by an adapter

title: Nx, Turborepo, TypeScript, and other BlueprintProvider adapters
trigger: the GraphReFly flagship is submission-ready and one external provider has a concrete fixture and fidelity contract

title: MCP server, GitHub App, hosted CI, and required-check adapters
trigger: the canonical CLI gate is stable and an approved integration can execute the same deterministic contract

title: automatic semantic approval and merge
trigger: post-MVP evidence defines policy, identity, authorization, rollback, and audit requirements

title: additional demo scenarios and production persistence
trigger: the flagship scenario is complete, judgeable, and submission-ready

title: DAG stacks, merge-parent selection, and partial-order review
trigger: the generic linear repository gate passes and the domain contract can add partial-order ancestry without changing WorkUnit identity

title: Stack-assisted semantic planning and plan-to-work-unit creation
trigger: generic repository review is stable and planning can emit ClaimDrafts without weakening deterministic acceptance

title: mutual exclusion, reservations, and concurrent-work ownership
trigger: stable WorkUnit identity and hosted or shared runner identity can prove reservation ownership, expiry, and override audit

title: proactive textual and semantic conflict avoidance
trigger: planning and reservation evidence can predict overlapping paths, Blueprint claims, dependencies, and policy conflicts before implementation

title: enforceable source and capability scopes for AI and human agents
trigger: authenticated actor identity and repository policy can bind allowed paths and GraphReFly capabilities to an auditable work unit

title: evidence-backed revert, rollback planning, and dependent-stack recovery
trigger: DAG lineage, immutable bindings, and policy-aware inverse or compensating changes can be validated without treating git revert success as semantic recovery

title: deadline-safe product polish after generic MVP proof
trigger: PRODUCT-1 and the first refreshed submission information are complete with enough time remaining before BW-D1 to preserve a final evidence and submission refresh

title: per-revision dependency installation, immutable package caching, and monorepo package selection
trigger: the generic linear review gate passes and a real compatible repository needs to review a range that changes package manifests or dependency locks
